title: lycopodium miniatum var. bolivianum
intro: Lycopodium miniatum var. bolivianum, also known as dwarf club moss, is a species of plant that belongs to the club moss family (Lycopodiaceae). It is native to the tropical regions of South America, particularly in Bolivia, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Lycopodium miniatum var. bolivianum are typically made from the dried leaves and stems, which can be infused in hot water or used in tinctures to leverage its medicinal benefits.
